policy.rule: Declare A -> B only when A changes B's behavior or result.
policy.coverage: Every source-state by relevant target-state case must be present. Use pairwise coverage across independent sources and full Cartesian coverage only for compound branches.

interactions.description: The active custom profile determines which profile receives saved Settings changes.

cases.expected_result: Only the first profile changes and reloads.
Only the second profile changes and reloads.

interactions.description: Settings search controls which navigation destinations remain available.

cases.expected_result: Only Advanced remains visible.
No page remains visible and no unrelated page is selected as a result.
All eight navigation destinations return.

interactions.description: Cancel must prevent the Settings save behavior in both clean and dirty states.

cases.expected_result: The settings file is byte-for-byte unchanged.
The edited value was not serialized and the old value reloads.

interactions.description: The selected Theme mode changes what Settings saves and the application applies.

cases.expected_result: System persists, applies, and reloads.
Light persists, applies, and reloads.
Dark persists, applies, and reloads.

interactions.description: The configured zero/positive timeout changes whether auto-close occurs.

cases.expected_result: The timer cancels the picker once.
The picker stays visible until a manual action.

interactions.description: The automatic-speech setting changes whether a completed model reply enters TTS playback.

cases.expected_result: No reply synthesis or playback occurs; manual read-aloud remains usable.
Stable reply segments synthesize and play in order.

interactions.description: Transcript-review mode changes whether voice input pauses in the picker before querying.

cases.expected_result: The picker opens and no query is sent until the reviewed transcript is accepted.
The transcript is queried immediately and the reply is stored.

interactions.description: Every local-file access mode changes the availability of every local-file tool.

cases.expected_result: Not granted.
Not granted.
Not granted.
Not granted.
Not granted.
Granted and executable.
Granted and executable.
Not granted.
Not granted.
Not granted.
Granted and executable.

Granted; mutation requires the approval path.
Granted; mutation requires the approval path.
Granted and executable.
Granted and executable.
Granted and executes without an approval prompt.
Granted and executes without an approval prompt.
Granted and executes without an approval prompt.

interactions.description: The active Dark theme determines which saved custom palette is applied to the running application.

cases.expected_result: The compact profile palette persists and its background is present in the live application stylesheet.
The roomy profile palette persists and its background is present in the live application stylesheet.
The scroll profile palette persists and its background is present in the live application stylesheet.
The snap profile palette persists and its background is present in the live application stylesheet.

interactions.description: The saved icon-size value changes both dimensions of the already-running floating icon.

cases.expected_result: The visible icon resizes to 48 x 48 without restarting.
The visible icon resizes to 64 x 64 without restarting.

The visible icon resizes to 96 x 96 without restarting.

interactions.description: Compound bubble appearance and timing states must all survive Save and update the existing bubble together.

cases.expected_result: Compact geometry, colors, low timing values, and both disabled flags persist and apply live.
Roomy geometry, colors, high timing values, disabled scrolling, and enabled snap persist and apply live.
Midrange geometry, colors, enabled scrolling, and disabled snap persist and apply live.
Large geometry, colors, enabled scrolling, and enabled snap persist and apply live.

interactions.description: Selecting the built-in profile changes the model routes and profile identity that Save persists and reloads.

cases.expected_result: Low setup persists on the default runtime profile and all three routes reopen on ChatGPT gpt-5.5.

interactions.description: The clean or dirty Settings state determines whether guided profile setup may open.

cases.expected_result: The real guided profile wizard opens and is usable.
The wizard stays closed and Settings asks the user to save or discard first.

interactions.description: The selected Settings page determines the exact state Reset Page is allowed to remove.

cases.expected_result: App state resets while every other page sentinel survives.
Connection state resets while every other page sentinel survives.
Model-routing state resets while every other page sentinel survives.
Voice/audio state resets and the STT refresh path runs while every other page sentinel survives.
Shortcut state resets while every other page sentinel survives.
Prompt state resets while every other page sentinel survives.
Advanced state resets while every other page sentinel survives.
The no-setting About branch succeeds without removing any other page sentinel.

interactions.description: How the installer terminates determines whether an immediate app restart is valid.

cases.expected_result: Restart is visible and its click reaches the application restart boundary.
Restart remains hidden after a failed install.
Restart remains hidden after user cancellation.

interactions.description: Failure versus user interruption changes the terminal detail that must remain in the optional-install artifacts.

cases.expected_result: The resolver output and durable detailed failure message remain on disk.
All output received before interruption and the cancellation marker remain on disk.

interactions.description: The packaged update-check result determines whether the download/apply flow becomes available.

cases.expected_result: The button becomes Download update and retains the selected asset.
No download is offered and the missing host build is explained.
The check action remains available and reports current status.
The check action becomes retryable and displays the failure.

interactions.description: Each download/apply outcome changes update status and whether the verified package remains retryable.

cases.expected_result: The error is visible and Download update is enabled for retry.
The verified path is retained and Apply update becomes available.
The verified path and Apply update action remain available.
The helper error is visible and Apply update remains retryable.
Applying is shown, the action disables, and app exit is scheduled.

interactions.description: The origin/main pull result determines the final repo-update status and retry state.

cases.expected_result: Settings requests a restart and restores Pull latest.
Settings reports up to date and restores Pull latest.
Settings displays the origin error and restores Pull latest for retry.

interactions.description: Successful crash ZIP creation determines the exact artifact revealed to the user.

cases.expected_result: Explorer/Finder receives the same existing ZIP that Settings names.

interactions.description: The crash bundle's exclusion policy determines what the user can inspect before sharing.

cases.expected_result: No chat sentinel appears in any archive member.
No memory sentinel appears in any archive member.
No settings or environment sentinel appears in any archive member.
No keychain sentinel appears and secret storage is never read.

interactions.description: Recorded runtime events populate and continue streaming into an open Runtime Status window.

cases.expected_result: The existing error and collapsed details appear when the tray action opens the window.
The warning streams into the existing tree and Copy all output.
Closing disables the live publisher so no dead window is targeted.

interactions.description: The user's exact-plan decision determines whether any Wisp-owned target is removed.

cases.expected_result: No helper starts and all planned targets remain.
Only the displayed packaged-app, data, optional-package, and model targets are removed.
Only the displayed source-checkout, data, optional-package, and model targets are removed.

interactions.description: Packaged versus source ownership changes the literal target list carried by the self-removing native helper.

cases.expected_result: The helper carries the packaged root and never a source checkout.
The helper carries the explicitly confirmed source root and source-deletion warning.

interactions.description: Selecting the macOS hotkey route changes registration ownership to the Carbon helper protocol.

cases.expected_result: The native entry accepts the helper readiness status and retains the live helper.
The helper JSON event becomes the exact native.hotkey add-on event.
The native stop entry terminates and forgets the helper.

interactions.description: Selecting the Linux desktop route changes hotkey registration to the pynput backend while retaining native-worker event semantics.

cases.expected_result: The configured caller binding emits its caller index.
The registered add-on binding emits its add-on and hotkey IDs.
The selected pynput backend stops and is forgotten.

interactions.description: Selecting the Windows desktop route changes hotkey registration to RegisterHotKey while retaining native-worker event semantics.

cases.expected_result: The configured caller binding emits its caller index.
The registered add-on binding emits its add-on and hotkey IDs.
The selected RegisterHotKey backend stops and is forgotten.

interactions.description: On macOS, native focused-text capture prefers Accessibility selected text without injecting clipboard shortcuts.

cases.expected_result: native.selected_text returns the AX selection directly.

interactions.description: The Linux display session determines whether focused text comes from Wayland AT-SPI or X11 PRIMARY.

cases.expected_result: native.selected_text returns the focused AT-SPI selection.
native.selected_text returns the active-owner PRIMARY selection.

interactions.description: On Windows, native focused-text capture uses UI Automation when clipboard fallback is disabled.

cases.expected_result: native.selected_text returns the UIA selection directly.

interactions.description: Clipboard retention policy affects every generated-text paste method.

cases.expected_result: The reply is pasted and remains on the clipboard.
The reply is pasted and the original clipboard is restored.
The cached UIA range is selected and receives the reply without a restore write.
The cached UIA range receives the reply and the original clipboard is restored.
AX writes in place and never touches the clipboard.
AX still writes in place without an unnecessary clipboard read or write.
The target app activates, receives Cmd+V, and the reply remains on the clipboard.
The target app receives Cmd+V and the original clipboard is restored.

interactions.description: macOS logical-point screen coordinates determine the capture region passed to the native capture backend.

cases.expected_result: The selected rectangle includes the virtual origin but is not multiplied by display scale.

interactions.description: Windows monitor origin and live DPI determine the physical capture region emitted by the overlay.

cases.expected_result: The region uses the negative virtual origin at 1x scale.
The same logical rectangle is recomputed with the new origin and doubled physical size.

interactions.description: First-run completion state and the final checkbox determine whether setup appears and whether it opens a new chat.

cases.expected_result: The fresh-install wizard completes, applies settings, and requests one forced-new chat.
The fresh-install wizard completes without requesting a chat.
The automatic wizard does not reopen and no first-chat request occurs.

interactions.description: Simple versus advanced navigation determines whether provider configuration is part of the setup path.

cases.expected_result: Continue skips the provider page and reaches optional sign-in.
Continue includes the provider/model/endpoint/key page before optional sign-in.

interactions.description: An explicit provider choice changes whether a successful ChatGPT sign-in owns the final model route.

cases.expected_result: The explicit provider remains selected despite successful sign-in.
The completed ChatGPT sign-in selects the ChatGPT provider.
No browser opens and the new personal profile retains its normal default provider.

interactions.description: TTS and STT preferences jointly determine whether setup starts no installer, one local installer, or the combined speech installer.

The STT preference persists and the Whisper preparation plan is scheduled.
Both preferences persist and no local installer is scheduled.
The TTS preference persists and the Kokoro preparation plan is scheduled.
Both local preferences persist and one combined speech preparation plan is scheduled.
Both preferences persist and only the Kokoro preparation plan is scheduled.
Both preferences persist and no local installer is scheduled.
Both preferences persist and only the Whisper preparation plan is scheduled.
Both cloud preferences persist and no local installer is scheduled.

interactions.description: Packaged versus source launch mode changes executable/module dispatch and code/data roots while preserving the same ready UI and four-worker runtime contract.

cases.expected_result: The frozen executable dispatches all four bundled worker modules and shows the overlay.
The packaged supervisor stops all bundled workers with no survivor.
The platform development launcher starts all four source workers and shows the overlay with isolated writable data.
The source supervisor stops all workers with no survivor.

interactions.description: Dragging the floating icon must carry the independently positioned provider controls badge with it.

cases.expected_result: The badge is centered below the initial icon position.
The badge moves to remain centered below the dragged icon.

interactions.description: Platform and enabled state determine which login artifact points at the proven source/packaged launch entry or whether no artifact remains.

interactions.description: Every rewrite-picker built-in must run from its painted clickable row.

cases.expected_result: Click emits the exact grammar prompt.
Click emits the exact simplify prompt.
Click emits the exact tone prompt.

interactions.description: The caller's paste-back mode changes the destination and side effects of the same real picker submission.

cases.expected_result: The real answer remains in the Wisp bubble and native paste is not called.
The real rewrite is sent to the captured app/focus target with clipboard restoration.

interactions.description: Every app-language state changes the visible label and hint for every localized built-in while leaving its assistant-language prompt intact.

cases.expected_result: All six rows use the effective system UI language and submit their assistant-language prompts.
All six rows display English and submit their assistant-language prompts.
All six rows display Simplified Chinese and submit their assistant-language prompts.
All six rows display Traditional Chinese and submit their assistant-language prompts.
All six rows display Spanish and submit their assistant-language prompts.

interactions.description: Every assistant-language state changes the submitted prompt for every built-in across every app language.

cases.expected_result: All six actions submit the default English templates in every app language.
All six actions submit neutral English templates while the system instruction matches the user language.
All six actions submit English prompts.
All six actions submit Simplified Chinese prompts.
All six actions submit Traditional Chinese prompts.
All six actions submit Spanish prompts.
All six actions submit French prompts.
All six actions submit German prompts.
All six actions submit Japanese prompts.
All six actions submit Korean prompts.
All six actions submit Portuguese prompts.
All six actions submit Hindi prompts.

interactions.description: Cancellation timing changes which stream output may reach the reply UI without contaminating a follow-up request.

cases.expected_result: The queued request is cooperatively cancelled before it can complete.
The real bubble Stop click terminates the active brain stream.
Late chunks and final text from the cancelled generation never reach the current UI.
A later request completes normally with its own correlated output.

interactions.description: A newer picker action supersedes an older stream, so rapid user input cannot mix generations.

cases.expected_result: The old request is marked cancellable and its parser remains private to that generation.
Submitting the newer intent cancels the older stream and starts a clean reply.
Deliberately late old thought, answer, done, and chat output are rejected.

interactions.description: The final model-route outcome determines whether the user sees an answer or a recovery recommendation.

cases.expected_result: The primary answer completes without a recovery notice.
The first successful configured fallback answer completes without a recovery notice.
The routed failure is rendered with a concrete retry and provider-limits recommendation.

interactions.description: Each shortcut's independent enabled state determines whether its primary and secondary bindings enter production registration.

cases.expected_result: The primary binding remains editable, loads into config, and is included in production registration.
The secondary binding remains editable, loads into config, and is included in production registration.
Only this row is disabled and its primary binding is omitted from registration.
Only this row is disabled and its secondary binding is omitted from registration.

interactions.description: Voice query, live voice, and dictation share one microphone, so rapid cross-mode input must preserve the first active owner.

cases.expected_result: Repeated voice_start events do not create a second recording.
Dictation start/repeat is ignored and its release cannot stop the voice-query recording.
Live voice refuses to start until the voice-query recording finishes.
Push-to-talk voice cannot claim the microphone.
The next live-voice toggle performs one orderly stop rather than starting another session.
Dictation cannot claim the microphone.
Stray F8/F9 release events do not affect the live session.
Voice-query start/repeat is ignored and its release cannot stop dictation.
Live voice refuses to start until dictation finishes.
Repeated dictate_start events do not create a second recording.
Only voice_stop ends the recording and routes the transcript to the assistant.
Only dictate_stop ends the recording and pastes its transcript.

interactions.description: The caller's persisted paste-back setting determines the completed answer destination.

cases.expected_result: The answer completes in Wisp without native paste.
The rewrite completes through the captured native paste target.

interactions.description: A caller's context modes and explicit tool overrides jointly determine the context and model-tool policy sent with its query.

cases.expected_result: The governed source is neither frontloaded nor exposed as a tool.
The governed source is collected before the model call.
The governed source's tools are offered to the model.
The explicit off override removes the tool even when a context dropdown could grant it.
The explicit on override allows and pins the tool.
The explicit model override allows the tool without pinning it.

interactions.description: Voice and snip each use their own saved context and tool policy rather than inheriting another caller's policy.

cases.expected_result: Voice builds query context from VOICE_* settings.
Voice receives its own allowed and pinned tool set.
Snip builds query context from SNIP_* settings while carrying the selected image.
Snip receives its own tool set without redundant screenshot capture.

interactions.description: Dictation mode changes transcript cleanup while preserving the original captured paste target.

cases.expected_result: The exact STT transcript is pasted without a model call.
The cleaned rewrite is pasted to the same PID and focus token.

interactions.description: Selection capture, clipboard paste, and icon drop all add persistent items that must appear in the next intent preview.

cases.expected_result: A Selection badge and preview are available to the next intent.
The pasted item appears as removable attachment context rather than typed path text.
The dropped text/image appears in the panel and is emitted into pending context.

interactions.description: Removing individual sources changes whether a context group stays enabled and what re-enabling restores.

cases.expected_result: Unrelated and remaining sources survive; re-enable restores the removed source set.
The empty group turns off, then re-enable restores its captured sources.

interactions.description: A numbered context-key toggle changes both the visible chip state and whether the source is attached at submission.

cases.expected_result: The chip visibly becomes off and a later preview refresh preserves that touched state.
The source content is excluded from the model request.
An available off source visibly becomes enabled.
The source's hotkey-time content is attached to the model request.

interactions.description: Root-folder and blocked-glob policy constrain every local-file access mode and operation.

cases.expected_result: Allowed read operations complete when the selected access mode grants them.
Allowed writes either request approval or complete automatically according to the selected mode.
Parent and symlink escapes are rejected before reading.
Parent and symlink escapes are rejected before mutation.

A matching private path is refused before mutation.

interactions.description: The selected snip mode changes the region emitted by the same visible overlay interaction.

cases.expected_result: A drag emits only the normalized and scaled rectangle.
A toolbar or canvas click emits the full virtual desktop.
The toolbar emits the current application bounds only when those bounds are valid.

interactions.description: The image attachment source changes the payload supplied to the same vision-query workflow.

cases.expected_result: The selected region bytes and current prompt reach the vision request.
The pasted bitmap bytes and current prompt reach the same vision request field.

interactions.description: Image-route health determines which configured model returns the vision answer without changing the image request.

cases.expected_result: The configured Image primary receives the prompt and image and returns the answer.
The first configured fallback receives the unchanged prompt and image.
The next fallback receives the unchanged request and its answer is returned.

interactions.description: Timestamp capability and the held speed control change how the same streamed reply is revealed and spoken.

cases.expected_result: Timestamp units drive reveal/highlight through the final word.
The WPM timer drains the stream instead of flushing it at reply.done.
Bubble and audio speed boost are enabled together.
Both return to normal speed exactly once.

interactions.description: The close control cancels active reply work but only dismisses informational notices.

cases.expected_result: The exact active model request and speech stop, late output is rejected, and UI returns idle.
The notice disappears without sending any backend cancellation.

interactions.description: Selection state determines which copy and UI Lab context actions appear and what text they receive.

Copy full still writes the complete bubble reply.
Edit/delete actions appear and receive the selected range.

cases.expected_result: The row appears in General.

cases.expected_result: No timer is active.
A 120-second daemon timer extracts facts on tick and starts the next timer.

interactions.description: The saved privacy mode changes the exact text and report that reach every model request.

cases.expected_result: Alice and the email reach the provider unchanged and no report is produced.
The email becomes EMAIL_1, Alice stays visible, and the reply is restored.
Alice becomes PERSON_1, the email becomes EMAIL_1, both categories are reported, and the reply is restored.

interactions.description: The privacy review decision determines whether redacted text, full text, or no request reaches the provider.

cases.expected_result: Only EMAIL_1 reaches the provider.
The original email reaches the provider for this request.
No provider call occurs and the request ends as a controlled cancellation.

interactions.description: Advanced mode adds local AI detections while retaining every non-overlapping built-in detection.

cases.expected_result: The built-in email category is detected.
The AI person and built-in email categories are both detected exactly once.

interactions.description: Advanced model installation status controls whether Install, Repair, Advanced use, or Remove is available.

cases.expected_result: Install is shown and Advanced cannot remain selected.
Repair is shown because the dedicated runtime is missing.
Advanced can be selected and Remove is visible.
The mode falls back to Built-in and Remove hides.

interactions.description: A successful OS-keychain save changes the provider field from typed plaintext to an empty masked stored marker on this and later Settings opens.

cases.expected_result: The Password field temporarily contains the user's typed key.
The key exists only at the keychain boundary and the field is cleared with a stored placeholder.
The provider row is reconstructed as empty, masked, and stored without plaintext settings.

interactions.description: Reset All removes both plain settings state and every supported stored credential after confirmation.

cases.expected_result: No settings or credential deletion occurs.
The settings file disappears, defaults reload, and API/OAuth keychain cleanup runs.

interactions.description: The one configured privacy policy applies before normal query/chat routing and to later tool results, independent of a cloud or local model adapter.

cases.expected_result: Sensitive prompt/context fields use stable placeholders.
Sensitive tool output reuses the session's placeholders before the next model call.
The privacy gate runs before provider selection, so local models follow the same policy.
